title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
abstract_excerpt: We introduce RAG models which combine pre-trained parametric and non-parametric memory for knowledge-intensive NLP tasks, retrieving relevant documents from Wikipedia and conditioning a seq2seq model on them.
relevance_note: Original RAG paper; defines the parametric + non-parametric memory architecture this mini-run examines for small-team workflows.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey
abstract_excerpt: This survey reviews the evolution of RAG paradigms (Naive, Advanced, Modular RAG) and the state of retrieval, generation, and augmentation techniques.
relevance_note: Survey grounding the taxonomy used to choose a Naive RAG baseline for the synthetic experiment in this mini-run.

title: In-Context Retrieval-Augmented Language Models
abstract_excerpt: We show that prepending retrieved documents to the input of an off-the-shelf language model — In-Context RALM — yields large perplexity gains across model sizes without retraining.
relevance_note: Justifies the in-context (no-finetune) RAG variant we benchmark against the no-retrieval baseline in this mini-run.
